about.title: Migration services in Brisbane you can trust
about.body: At MinRosh Migration, we help individuals and families navigate the Australian visa process with confidence and clarity. We understand that every case is different, so we focus on structured guidance, practical planning, and clear communication rather than generic one-size-fits-all advice.
points: Registered migration guidance for skilled migration, student, partner, and employer-sponsored pathways
Personalised visa strategies aligned to your background, timing, and goals
Structured preparation before formal case review, nomination planning, and visa lodgement

pathwaySteps.title: Initial Assessment
pathwaySteps.description: Check your age, points profile, occupation fit, and likely pathway options.
pathwaySteps.detail: The first stage is not about lodging anything immediately. It is about confirming whether your occupation aligns with skilled lists, whether your points position is realistic for the subclass you prefer, and whether employer-sponsored or family routes might be stronger. Many skilled professionals from Sri Lanka start here before committing to English tests or skills assessment fees. Use the Department of Home Affairs visa listing to see which visa subclasses exist and read the official eligibility summaries before you lock in a strategy.

pathwaySteps.title: Skills Assessment
pathwaySteps.description: Verify qualifications and employment evidence with the relevant assessing authority.
pathwaySteps.detail: Each assessing authority publishes occupation-specific criteria. You will usually need qualification transcripts, employment references that match the authority’s format, and sometimes additional evidence such as payment records or organisational charts. A positive skills assessment is a prerequisite for many skilled visas, so sequencing this step before English tests or EOI submission often saves time and cost.

pathwaySteps.title: English Test & EOI
pathwaySteps.description: Strengthen your points profile and prepare a competitive Expression of Interest.
pathwaySteps.detail: English scores can swing your points total significantly. Once you have a valid test and skills assessment (where required), you can submit an Expression of Interest in SkillSelect for relevant subclasses. Remember that submitting an EOI is not a visa application — you must be invited before you can lodge a points-tested skilled visa.

pathwaySteps.title: State Nomination
pathwaySteps.description: Explore nomination pathways that can lift your competitiveness for 190 or regional options.
pathwaySteps.detail: States and territories run their own nomination programs with occupation lists, minimum points, and sometimes work or residence requirements. Subclass 190 adds five points; subclass 491 regional nomination adds fifteen points but comes with regional residence obligations. Compare programs across states that match your occupation and personal circumstances rather than assuming one size fits all.

pathwaySteps.title: Visa Lodgement
pathwaySteps.description: Prepare the final application package with a clean, consistent document set.
pathwaySteps.detail: After invitation or when your visa stream does not use SkillSelect, lodgement requires form accuracy, consistent dates across documents, health examinations, police certificates, and payment of the visa application charge. Weak or contradictory evidence is a common cause of delays or requests for further information, so a final consistency review matters.

faq.question: How long does an Australian visa take?
faq.answer: Processing times vary depending on the visa category and the quality of the application. Some student visas may be processed faster, while partner and skilled pathways can take much longer depending on evidence, nomination, and government processing times.

faq.question: Do I need a migration agent?
faq.answer: A migration agent is not legally required for every application, but professional guidance can reduce errors, improve document quality, and help you avoid weak strategy decisions before you lodge.

faq.question: How much does an Australian visa cost?
faq.answer: Visa application charges vary by visa type, and there can also be costs for English tests, skills assessments, health checks, and supporting documents. Contact MinRosh Migration for a clearer estimate based on your situation.

faq.question: How do I get PR in Australia in 2026?
faq.answer: Permanent residence depends on your visa pathway. Common routes include skilled points-tested visas (such as subclasses 189, 190, or 491), employer-sponsored pathways that may lead to permanent options where eligible, partner visas, and some graduate or specialist routes. You usually need to meet health, character, and specific visa criteria, and skilled routes often require a positive skills assessment and competitive English. Check the Department of Home Affairs visa listing for the subclass that matches your situation, then map nomination, residence, and evidence requirements before you lodge.

faq.question: What are the 190 visa requirements for Queensland in 2026?
faq.answer: Subclass 190 is a state-nominated skilled visa. Queensland runs its own nomination program with occupation lists, minimum points, and work or residence conditions that change over time. You must also meet Home Affairs eligibility for subclass 190, including skills assessment and English where required. Always read the current Queensland migration program pages and the official 190 visa page before planning, because lists and rules are updated regularly.

faq.question: What is the Skills in Demand visa in Australia?
faq.answer: Skills in Demand refers to Australia’s employer-sponsored skilled framework that groups roles into streams such as Specialist Skills, Core Skills, and Essential Skills, with different settings for occupations and income thresholds. It replaces the older single-number branding many people still call the 482. The exact name, lists, and rules on the Department of Home Affairs website are authoritative — use those when deciding whether your job fits a sponsored pathway.

faq.question: What is the minimum salary for employer sponsored visa Australia 2026?
faq.answer: Employer-sponsored skilled nominations must meet the Temporary Skilled Migration Income Threshold (TSMIT) that applies when the nomination is decided, together with any other income rules for your visa stream. TSMIT is updated over time, including typical annual indexation from 1 July. Do not rely on old blog figures: confirm the current threshold and exemptions on the Department of Home Affairs employer-sponsored visa pages before you accept a job offer or lodge.

faq.question: How do I get a PR visa in Queensland as a nurse in 2026?
faq.answer: As a nurse you would usually start by confirming your occupation and skills assessment pathway (often through ANMAC or the relevant nursing authority), meeting English requirements, and checking whether you will pursue employer-sponsored options (Skills in Demand / permanent routes where eligible) or points-tested skilled visas (189, 190, or 491). Many Sri Lankan-trained nurses map to Australian nursing occupations when registration and assessment align — but you must verify your own pathway against current lists and AHPRA requirements. Queensland runs its own state and regional nomination programs with lists and rules that change over time. Read the current Queensland migration program material and the Department of Home Affairs visa pages for subclasses 190 and 491, then map health and character requirements before you lodge.

faq.question: Where is the official list of Australian visas?
faq.answer: The Department of Home Affairs publishes the visa listing at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au under Getting a visa. It is the authoritative catalogue of subclasses. Use it to open the page for the visa you are considering, then read criteria, charges, and forms there — summaries on blogs or social media can go out of date quickly.

faq.question: What is the Department of Home Affairs Visa Finder?
faq.answer: Visa Finder is an official tool that helps you explore visa options by answering questions about your situation. It is useful for early orientation, but it does not replace reading the legal criteria for the specific subclass you intend to apply for. Always cross-check results on the visa listing and the subclass page before lodging.

faq.question: How can I spend more time understanding visas before I book?
faq.answer: Start with the Department of Home Affairs visa listing and Visa Finder, then read MinRosh guides such as the official sources hub, skilled points guide, student requirements guide, and document checklist. When you are ready for sequencing and evidence review tailored to your facts, use the free assessment or book a consultation.
